name: FII (Foreign Institutional Investor) Flows — India
summary: Created 2026-05-12. **DAY 2 EXTENSION (May 13): FIIs CONTINUED NET SELLERS May 12 (multiple outlets confirm continuation rather than reversal of May 11 ₹8,437 cr outflow pattern); Moody's CUT India FY26 GDP 0.8PP TO 6.0% adds SOVEREIGN-RATING channel to structural FII case; Sensex closed May 12 at 74,559.24 (-1,456 pts, -1.92%) = largest 2-session drawdown of cycle; Hardeep Puri quantified OMC pain at ₹1,000 cr/day = fiscal-deficit channel quantified.** May 13 intraday relief bounce in Nifty/Sensex is technical not fundamental — cumulative drawdown -1.83% from May 11 peak. RBI reserves drawdown next print due ~May 16. FII (Foreign Institutional Investor) net cash-equity flows into Indian listed equities are the **TRANSMISSION MECHANISM** in every India-market causal chain of the cycle. Tracked via NSDL/NSE primary daily prints. **MAY 11 FII SINGLE-DAY NET SELL ₹8,437.56 cr (highest since April 24); DII +₹5,939.65 cr absorbed ~70%; YTD CY26 FII outflow ₹2.06 LAKH CR — already exceeds full-year 2025 ₹1.66 lakh cr by ₹40k cr (+₹14k cr in just 2 sessions May 11-12).** Drivers: Trump rejection of Iran counter-proposal + Brent $105.58 + INR breach of ₹95.50 RBI threshold. **The cascade is now self-reinforcing: weaker rupee → imported inflation → bond yields up (G-Sec 6.96-6.99%) → debt-FPI also out → more INR pressure → more FII exit.** DII absorption (~₹1.7 lakh cr YTD via SIP) covering ~70-90% of FII selling depending on day; gap WIDENING. FPI ownership of Indian equities at ~16%, lowest in nearly two decades. **STRUCTURAL CRACK POINT: if FII outflows accelerate past ₹2.5 lakh cr by end-Q2, DII channel cracks.** Sonal Varma (Nomura): 'Don't expect any sustained pick-up in portfolio flows, as structural concerns over AI-related disruption to India's software industry remain'; recent RBI restrictions on AD forex hedging have increased FX hedge costs, deterring portfolio inflows; sees 'further scope for the rupee to depreciate.'

current.ytd_cy26_fii_outflow: ₹2.06 lakh cr (exceeds full-year 2025 ₹1.66 lakh cr by ₹40k cr)

active_triggers.condition: FII YTD CY26 outflow exceeds ₹2.5 lakh cr by end-Q2 (by 2026-06-30) — DII channel cracks
FII flow turn POSITIVE for 5 consecutive sessions — confirms India deployment-zone re-entry

top_edges.note: bank-nifty is sub-index inside nifty-50 node; edge captures specific bank-financials transmission

recent_signals.headline: FII SINGLE-DAY NET SELL ₹8,437.56 cr MAY 11 = highest since April 24. DII +₹5,939.65 cr absorbed ~70% (₹5,939.65 cr). YTD CY26 FII outflow ₹2.06 LAKH CR — exceeds full-year 2025 (₹1.66 lakh cr) by ₹40k cr; added ₹14k cr in just 2 sessions May 11-12. Drivers: Trump rejection 'TOTALLY UNACCEPTABLE' + Brent $105.58 + INR ₹95.54 fresh record breaking RBI ₹95.50 threshold + WH Sit-Room weighing renewed strikes. Cascade self-reinforcing: weaker rupee → imported inflation → bond yields up → debt-FPI out → more INR pressure. RBI forex reserves -$7.79bn week ended May 1 to $690.69bn = evidence of active intervention not stopping the move. Sonal Varma (Nomura) flagging 'further depreciation' on AI-disruption + RBI hedging restrictions deterring inflows.
sources: Business Standard May 11 + May 12 live blogs
Republic World / Outlook Money (₹2.06 lakh cr tally)
recent_signals.verification: FII -₹8,437.56 cr May 11 = CONFIRMED (NSDL primary); DII +₹5,939.65 cr = CONFIRMED (NSDL primary); YTD ₹2.06 lakh cr = CONFIRMED multi-source (Business Standard + Outlook Money); RBI reserves -$7.79bn = CONFIRMED (RBI primary)
